examples.title: 2:1 Multiplexer
examples.code:
ALGORITHM MUX_2to1
INPUTS: I0, I1 (data inputs), S (select line)
OUTPUT: Y
BEGIN
  IF S = 0 THEN
    Y ← I0
  ELSE
    Y ← I1
  END IF
  RETURN Y
END
examples.explanation: When select line S is 0, input I0 is selected and passed to output Y. When S is 1, input I1 is selected. This is the fundamental operation of a 2:1 multiplexer.

examples.title: 4:1 Multiplexer Implementation
examples.code:
def mux_4to1(I, S):
    """
    4:1 Multiplexer Implementation
    I: list of 4 data inputs [I0, I1, I2, I3]
    S: select lines as integer 0-3
    Returns selected input
    """
    if S == 0:
        return I[0]  # Select I0 when S=00
    elif S == 1:
        return I[1]  # Select I1 when S=01
    elif S == 2:
        return I[2]  # Select I2 when S=10
    elif S == 3:
        return I[3]  # Select I3 when S=11

# Example usage
inputs = [0, 1, 0, 1]  # I0=0, I1=1, I2=0, I3=1
select = 3  # Binary 11 selects input I3
output = mux_4to1(inputs, select)
print(f"Output: {output}")  # Prints: Output: 1
examples.explanation: This Python implementation treats select lines as a binary number that indexes into the input array. The function returns the input at the index specified by the select lines.

examples.title: 8:1 Multiplexer in C
examples.description: C program implementing 8:1 MUX with enable input
examples.explanation: This C implementation demonstrates an 8:1 MUX with enable functionality. The select lines (0-7) index into the inputs array to select one of 8 data inputs. The enable pin can disable the MUX output.

examples.title: 4:1 Multiplexer VHDL Code
examples.description: Structural VHDL model of 4:1 MUX using dataflow style
examples.explanation: This VHDL code describes a 4:1 multiplexer at behavioral level. The case statement maps select line combinations to specific inputs from the 4-bit input vector. This is typical HDL modeling for digital synthesis.
